BCW

Aine

2026-03-05

Loading Libraries:

Loading Dataset:

#Breast Cancer Wisconsin (Diagnostic)
BCWdata <- read.csv("C:wisconsin_dbc.csv")

Review Dataset:

BCW <- BCWdata %>%
  dplyr::select(-"ID") %>%                                    # removing the ID
  mutate(malignant = ifelse(Diagnosis == "M", 1, 0)) %>%  #numeric
  dplyr::select(-"Diagnosis")                                 
summary(BCW)
##   Radius_mean      Texture_mean   Perimeter_mean     Area_mean     
##  Min.   : 6.981   Min.   : 9.71   Min.   : 43.79   Min.   : 143.5  
##  1st Qu.:11.700   1st Qu.:16.17   1st Qu.: 75.17   1st Qu.: 420.3  
##  Median :13.370   Median :18.84   Median : 86.24   Median : 551.1  
##  Mean   :14.127   Mean   :19.29   Mean   : 91.97   Mean   : 654.9  
##  3rd Qu.:15.780   3rd Qu.:21.80   3rd Qu.:104.10   3rd Qu.: 782.7  
##  Max.   :28.110   Max.   :39.28   Max.   :188.50   Max.   :2501.0  
##  Smoothness_mean   Compactness_mean  Concavity_mean    Nconcave_mean    
##  Min.   :0.05263   Min.   :0.01938   Min.   :0.00000   Min.   :0.00000  
##  1st Qu.:0.08637   1st Qu.:0.06492   1st Qu.:0.02956   1st Qu.:0.02031  
##  Median :0.09587   Median :0.09263   Median :0.06154   Median :0.03350  
##  Mean   :0.09636   Mean   :0.10434   Mean   :0.08880   Mean   :0.04892  
##  3rd Qu.:0.10530   3rd Qu.:0.13040   3rd Qu.:0.13070   3rd Qu.:0.07400  
##  Max.   :0.16340   Max.   :0.34540   Max.   :0.42680   Max.   :0.20120  
##  Symmetry_mean    Fractaldim_mean     Radius_se        Texture_se    
##  Min.   :0.1060   Min.   :0.04996   Min.   :0.1115   Min.   :0.3602  
##  1st Qu.:0.1619   1st Qu.:0.05770   1st Qu.:0.2324   1st Qu.:0.8339  
##  Median :0.1792   Median :0.06154   Median :0.3242   Median :1.1080  
##  Mean   :0.1812   Mean   :0.06280   Mean   :0.4052   Mean   :1.2169  
##  3rd Qu.:0.1957   3rd Qu.:0.06612   3rd Qu.:0.4789   3rd Qu.:1.4740  
##  Max.   :0.3040   Max.   :0.09744   Max.   :2.8730   Max.   :4.8850  
##   Perimeter_se       Area_se        Smoothness_se      Compactness_se    
##  Min.   : 0.757   Min.   :  6.802   Min.   :0.001713   Min.   :0.002252  
##  1st Qu.: 1.606   1st Qu.: 17.850   1st Qu.:0.005169   1st Qu.:0.013080  
##  Median : 2.287   Median : 24.530   Median :0.006380   Median :0.020450  
##  Mean   : 2.866   Mean   : 40.337   Mean   :0.007041   Mean   :0.025478  
##  3rd Qu.: 3.357   3rd Qu.: 45.190   3rd Qu.:0.008146   3rd Qu.:0.032450  
##  Max.   :21.980   Max.   :542.200   Max.   :0.031130   Max.   :0.135400  
##   Concavity_se      Nconcave_se        Symmetry_se       Fractaldim_se      
##  Min.   :0.00000   Min.   :0.000000   Min.   :0.007882   Min.   :0.0008948  
##  1st Qu.:0.01509   1st Qu.:0.007638   1st Qu.:0.015160   1st Qu.:0.0022480  
##  Median :0.02589   Median :0.010930   Median :0.018730   Median :0.0031870  
##  Mean   :0.03189   Mean   :0.011796   Mean   :0.020542   Mean   :0.0037949  
##  3rd Qu.:0.04205   3rd Qu.:0.014710   3rd Qu.:0.023480   3rd Qu.:0.0045580  
##  Max.   :0.39600   Max.   :0.052790   Max.   :0.078950   Max.   :0.0298400  
##  Radius_extreme  Texture_extreme Perimeter_extreme  Area_extreme   
##  Min.   : 7.93   Min.   :12.02   Min.   : 50.41    Min.   : 185.2  
##  1st Qu.:13.01   1st Qu.:21.08   1st Qu.: 84.11    1st Qu.: 515.3  
##  Median :14.97   Median :25.41   Median : 97.66    Median : 686.5  
##  Mean   :16.27   Mean   :25.68   Mean   :107.26    Mean   : 880.6  
##  3rd Qu.:18.79   3rd Qu.:29.72   3rd Qu.:125.40    3rd Qu.:1084.0  
##  Max.   :36.04   Max.   :49.54   Max.   :251.20    Max.   :4254.0  
##  Smoothness_extreme Compactness_extreme Concavity_extreme Nconcave_extreme 
##  Min.   :0.07117    Min.   :0.02729     Min.   :0.0000    Min.   :0.00000  
##  1st Qu.:0.11660    1st Qu.:0.14720     1st Qu.:0.1145    1st Qu.:0.06493  
##  Median :0.13130    Median :0.21190     Median :0.2267    Median :0.09993  
##  Mean   :0.13237    Mean   :0.25427     Mean   :0.2722    Mean   :0.11461  
##  3rd Qu.:0.14600    3rd Qu.:0.33910     3rd Qu.:0.3829    3rd Qu.:0.16140  
##  Max.   :0.22260    Max.   :1.05800     Max.   :1.2520    Max.   :0.29100  
##  Symmetry_extreme Fractaldim_extreme   malignant     
##  Min.   :0.1565   Min.   :0.05504    Min.   :0.0000  
##  1st Qu.:0.2504   1st Qu.:0.07146    1st Qu.:0.0000  
##  Median :0.2822   Median :0.08004    Median :0.0000  
##  Mean   :0.2901   Mean   :0.08395    Mean   :0.3726  
##  3rd Qu.:0.3179   3rd Qu.:0.09208    3rd Qu.:1.0000  
##  Max.   :0.6638   Max.   :0.20750    Max.   :1.0000
cor_mat <- cor(BCW[sapply(BCW, is.numeric)], use="pairwise.complete.obs")
cor_mat <- as.matrix(cor_mat)
ggcorrplot(cor_mat)
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggcorrplot package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/kassambara/ggcorrplot/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

correlation = as.data.frame(as.table(cor_mat)) %>%
  mutate(Var1 = as.character(Var1), Var2 = as.character(Var2)) %>% #lets filter duplicates out 
  filter(Var1 < Var2) %>% #removes duplicates
  filter(abs(Freq) > 0.7) %>% 
  arrange(desc(abs(Freq))) #decending order
kable(correlation, digits = 2, caption = "Correlations greater than .7")
Correlations greater than .7
Var1 Var2 Freq
Perimeter_mean Radius_mean 1.00
Perimeter_extreme Radius_extreme 0.99
Area_mean Radius_mean 0.99

Radius_extreme Radius_se 0.72
Nconcave_mean Perimeter_se 0.71
Area_mean malignant 0.71 
total_correlation <- nrow(correlation)
total_correlation
## [1] 78

Split into Test/ Train:

set.seed(321321) #reproducible
n<-nrow(BCW)
train.BCW<-runif(n)<0.7
BCW.test<-BCW[!train.BCW,]
V2.test<-BCW.test$malignant
V2.test.factor <- as.factor(BCW.test$malignant)

Create Reproducible Brier Score:

brier_score <- function(y, pred_prob) {
  mean((y - pred_prob)^2)
}

Logistic Regression:

Brier’s Score:

glm.BCW<-glm(malignant ~., data = BCW, subset = train.BCW, family = binomial)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
BCW.probs<-predict(glm.BCW, newdata = BCW.test, type = "response") 
GLM_Brier = brier_score(V2.test, BCW.probs)
GLM_Brier
## [1] 0.03488372

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy Score:

Threshold: A threshold of .3 was chosen to ensure high sensitivity with respect to specify. A threshold of .3 helps reduce the risk of false negatives which would have devastating consequences with respect to classifying tumors.

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy:

glm.pred <- rep(0, nrow(BCW.test)) #size of the test set.
glm.pred[BCW.probs > 0.3] = 1#.3threshold for classification
table(glm.pred, V2.test)
##         V2.test
## glm.pred   0   1
##        0 109   2
##        1   4  57
GLM.Accuracy = mean(glm.pred == V2.test)
GLM.Accuracy
## [1] 0.9651163

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.glm <- roc(V2.test, BCW.probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.glm, type = "s")

GLM.AUC = auc(roc_obj.glm)
GLM.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9654

Calibration CUrve:

valProbggplot(BCW.probs, V2.test)
## optimization did not converge for lrm.fit
## Step size  0.0078125  has reduced below minstepsize= 0.01 without improving log likelihood; fitting stopped
## convergence code 1
## Warning in valProbggplot(BCW.probs, V2.test): The lrm function did not converge
## when computing the calibration intercept!
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : pseudoinverse used at -0.005
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : neighborhood radius 1.005
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : reciprocal condition number 0
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : There are other near singularities as well. 1.01
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : pseudoinverse used at
## -0.005
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : neighborhood radius
## 1.005
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : reciprocal condition
## number 0
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : There are other near
## singularities as well. 1.01

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = BCW.probs, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##          Dxy      C (ROC)           R2            D     D:Chi-sq          D:p 
##   0.93070346   0.96535173   0.87247122   0.99210084 171.64134496   0.00000000 
##            U     U:Chi-sq          U:p            Q        Brier    Intercept 
##   2.21491948 382.96615102   0.00000000  -1.22281864   0.03488372           NA 
##        Slope         Emax Brier scaled         Eavg          ECI 
##   0.09231802   0.55745033   0.84520774   0.03488372   0.17344832

Stepwise Appraoches:

Using stepwise approach and choosing ‘both’ allowed the model to evaluate predictors using both forward and backward steps. It’s final model includes only 13 predictors. It’s classification ability is evaluated below:

set.seed(123)
glm.step <- step(glm.BCW, direction = "both", trace = FALSE) # Stepwise (both forward & backward) selection based on AIC. FALSE= dont see how got to choosen values. 
summary(glm.step)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = malignant ~ Radius_mean + Texture_mean + Area_mean + 
##     Compactness_mean + Concavity_mean + Nconcave_mean + Radius_se + 
##     Smoothness_se + Compactness_se + Concavity_se + Nconcave_se + 
##     Symmetry_se + Fractaldim_se + Radius_extreme + Smoothness_extreme + 
##     Compactness_extreme + Concavity_extreme + Symmetry_extreme + 
##     Fractaldim_extreme, family = binomial, data = BCW, subset = train.BCW)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept)         -1.542e+03  8.375e+04  -0.018    0.985
## Radius_mean         -2.151e+03  3.720e+04  -0.058    0.954
## Texture_mean         1.635e+02  2.587e+03   0.063    0.950
## Area_mean            2.272e+01  3.846e+02   0.059    0.953
## Compactness_mean    -3.282e+04  5.396e+05  -0.061    0.952
## Concavity_mean       1.527e+04  2.500e+05   0.061    0.951
## Nconcave_mean        5.398e+04  9.458e+05   0.057    0.954
## Radius_se            3.911e+03  6.308e+04   0.062    0.951
## Smoothness_se       -2.393e+05  3.826e+06  -0.063    0.950
## Compactness_se       1.031e+05  1.629e+06   0.063    0.950
## Concavity_se        -4.363e+04  6.999e+05  -0.062    0.950
## Nconcave_se          1.259e+05  2.010e+06   0.063    0.950
## Symmetry_se         -5.981e+04  9.881e+05  -0.061    0.952
## Fractaldim_se       -9.213e+05  1.459e+07  -0.063    0.950
## Radius_extreme       2.914e+02  5.416e+03   0.054    0.957
## Smoothness_extreme   1.614e+04  2.613e+05   0.062    0.951
## Compactness_extreme -1.211e+04  1.926e+05  -0.063    0.950
## Concavity_extreme    5.347e+03  8.572e+04   0.062    0.950
## Symmetry_extreme     1.086e+04  1.781e+05   0.061    0.951
## Fractaldim_extreme   9.398e+04  1.482e+06   0.063    0.949
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 5.2931e+02  on 396  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 1.5402e-05  on 377  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 40
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 25

Brier Score:

step.probs<-predict(glm.step, newdata = BCW.test, type = "response") 
step_Brier = brier_score(V2.test, step.probs) 
step_Brier
## [1] 0.02325581

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy Score:

BCW.step.pred <- rep(0, nrow(BCW.test)) #size of the test set.
BCW.step.pred[step.probs > 0.3] = 1 #.5threshold for classification
table(BCW.step.pred, V2.test)
##              V2.test
## BCW.step.pred   0   1
##             0 110   1
##             1   3  58
Accuracy.step = mean(BCW.step.pred == V2.test)
Accuracy.step
## [1] 0.9767442

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.step <- roc(V2.test, step.probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.step, type = "s")

step.AUC = auc(roc_obj.step)
step.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9783

Calibration CUrve:

valProbggplot(step.probs, V2.test)
## optimization did not converge for lrm.fit
## Step size  0.0078125  has reduced below minstepsize= 0.01 without improving log likelihood; fitting stopped
## convergence code 1
## Warning in valProbggplot(step.probs, V2.test): The lrm function did not
## converge when computing the calibration intercept!
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : pseudoinverse used at -0.005
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : neighborhood radius 1.005
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : reciprocal condition number 0
## Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
## : There are other near singularities as well. 1.01
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : pseudoinverse used at
## -0.005
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : neighborhood radius
## 1.005
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : reciprocal condition
## number 0
## Warning in predLoess(object$y, object$x, newx = if (is.null(newdata)) object$x
## else if (is.data.frame(newdata))
## as.matrix(model.frame(delete.response(terms(object)), : There are other near
## singularities as well. 1.01

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = step.probs, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##          Dxy      C (ROC)           R2            D     D:Chi-sq          D:p 
##   0.95650217   0.97825109   0.91290581   1.07479920 185.86546192   0.00000000 
##            U     U:Chi-sq          U:p            Q        Brier    Intercept 
##   1.45939334 253.01565442   0.00000000  -0.38459414   0.02325581           NA 
##        Slope         Emax Brier scaled         Eavg          ECI 
##   0.10629210   0.59273810   0.89680516   0.02325581   0.09101744
STEP <- data.frame(Model = c("Brier Score", 'Accuracy Score', 'Area Under the Curve'), STEP = c(step_Brier,Accuracy.step,step.AUC))

STEP %>%
  kable(caption = "Stepwise Subset Selection", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
Stepwise Subset Selection
Model STEP
Brier Score 0.023
Accuracy Score 0.977
Area Under the Curve 0.978

Ridge Regression:

set.seed(123)
x <- model.matrix(malignant ~ ., BCW)[, -1] #make matrixes
y <- BCW$malignant
ridge.mod<-glmnet(x[train.BCW,], y[train.BCW], alpha = 0, family = binomial)
cv.out <- cv.glmnet(x[train.BCW, ], y[train.BCW], alpha = 0, family = binomial)
plot(cv.out)

bestlam <- cv.out$lambda.min #May prefer 1-SE not minimum but here minimum. 
bestlam
## [1] 0.03832769
#cv.out$lambda.1se?

Brier Score:

ridge.probs <- predict(ridge.mod, s = bestlam,newx = x[!train.BCW, ], type = "response")
Ridge_Brier = brier_score(V2.test, ridge.probs)
Ridge_Brier
## [1] 0.01899515

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy Score:

BCW.ridge.pred <- rep(0, nrow(BCW.test)) #size of the test set.
BCW.ridge.pred[ridge.probs > .3] <- 1 #.3threshold for classification
ridge.probs <- as.vector(ridge.probs)
table(BCW.ridge.pred, V2.test)
##               V2.test
## BCW.ridge.pred   0   1
##              0 108   1
##              1   5  58
Accuracy.Ridge = mean(BCW.ridge.pred == V2.test)
Accuracy.Ridge
## [1] 0.9651163

ROC Curve:

roc_obj.ridge <- roc(V2.test, ridge.probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.ridge, type = "s")

Ridge.AUC = auc(roc_obj.ridge)
Ridge.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9987

Calibration Plot:

Ridge.calplot<-valProbggplot(ridge.probs, V2.test)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
Ridge.calplot

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = ridge.probs, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##           Dxy       C (ROC)            R2             D      D:Chi-sq 
##  9.973001e-01  9.986501e-01  9.663441e-01  1.195770e+00  2.066725e+02 
##           D:p             U      U:Chi-sq           U:p             Q 
##  0.000000e+00  6.881371e-02  1.383596e+01  9.898285e-04  1.126957e+00 
##         Brier     Intercept         Slope          Emax  Brier scaled 
##  1.899515e-02 -2.223044e-01  3.014396e+00  2.665711e-01  9.157113e-01 
##          Eavg           ECI 
##  4.091567e-02  4.004702e-01

Lasso Model:

set.seed(321321)
lasso.mod<-glmnet(x[train.BCW,], y[train.BCW], alpha = 1, family = binomial)
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge

## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
cv.out <- cv.glmnet(x[train.BCW, ], y[train.BCW], alpha = 1, family = binomial)
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge

## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge

## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge

## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge

## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glmnet.fit: algorithm did not converge
plot(cv.out)

bestlam <- cv.out$lambda.min #May prefer 1-SE not minimum but here minimum.
bestlam
## [1] 0.003333547
#cv.out$lambda.1se
Kept <- coef(cv.out, s = "lambda.min")
Kept[Kept[,1] != 0, ]
##        (Intercept)      Nconcave_mean      Symmetry_mean          Radius_se 
##       -28.27164087        19.78024697         0.75702156         8.46947873 
##     Compactness_se      Fractaldim_se     Radius_extreme    Texture_extreme 
##       -41.41113283      -139.33046881         0.42895650         0.21205314 
##  Perimeter_extreme Smoothness_extreme  Concavity_extreme   Nconcave_extreme 
##         0.03890922        20.53102750         5.13413876        16.53568208 
##   Symmetry_extreme 
##         8.29722177

Brier’s Score:

lasso.probs <- predict(lasso.mod, s = bestlam,newx = x[!train.BCW, ], type = "response")
lasso_Brier = brier_score(V2.test, ridge.probs)#Ensure factors are 1 & 0. 
lasso_Brier
## [1] 0.01899515

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy Score:

BCW.lasso.pred <- rep(0, nrow(BCW.test)) #size of the test set.
BCW.lasso.pred[lasso.probs > .3] <- 1 #.3threshold for classification
table(BCW.lasso.pred, V2.test)
##               V2.test
## BCW.lasso.pred   0   1
##              0 111   1
##              1   2  58
Accuracy.lasso = mean(BCW.lasso.pred == V2.test)
Accuracy.lasso
## [1] 0.9825581

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.lasso <- roc(V2.test, ridge.probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.lasso, type = "s")

lasso.AUC = auc(roc_obj.lasso)
lasso.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9987

Calibration Plot:

Lasso.calplot<-valProbggplot(lasso.probs, V2.test)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
Lasso.calplot

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = lasso.probs, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##           Dxy       C (ROC)            R2             D      D:Chi-sq 
##   0.997000150   0.998500075   0.965450637   1.193622578 206.303083345 
##           D:p             U      U:Chi-sq           U:p             Q 
##   0.000000000   0.005229305   2.899440420   0.234635928   1.188393273 
##         Brier     Intercept         Slope          Emax  Brier scaled 
##   0.012985545  -0.206453104   1.633638925   0.114009040   0.942378226 
##          Eavg           ECI 
##   0.016867781   0.105324431

Matrix Test/ Train:

set.seed(123)
standardized.X <- scale(BCW[, names(BCW) != "malignant"]) #standardize & sure malignant isnt 
Y <- as.factor(BCW$malignant)#y must be a factor in PCA
X <- standardized.X[train.BCW, ]
XTest <- standardized.X[!train.BCW, ]
Ytrain <- Y[train.BCW]
Ytest <- Y[!train.BCW]
Ytest_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(Ytest)) #going factor-numeric causes it to fail
Ytrain_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(Ytrain))

PCA Approach:

plsda.fit <- plsda(X, Ytrain, cv = TRUE)
validationplot(plsda.fit, val.type = "MSEP")

From this plot we can wee that having one PCA component preforms best on the training data. Brier’s Score

plsda.fit <- plsda(X, Ytrain, ncomp = 1)
plsda.probs <- predict(plsda.fit, newdata = XTest, type = "prob")

prob_M <- plsda.probs[, "1", 1]
Ytest_numeric <- as.numeric(as.character(Ytest))#becuase made y a factor back to numeric

plsda_Brier <- brier_score(Ytest_numeric, prob_M)
plsda_Brier
## [1] 0.1169014

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy Score:

BCW.plsda.pred <- rep(0, length(prob_M))
BCW.plsda.pred[prob_M > 0.3] <- 1
table(BCW.plsda.pred, Ytest)
##               Ytest
## BCW.plsda.pred  0  1
##              0 47  0
##              1 66 59
Accuracy.plsda = mean(BCW.plsda.pred == Ytest)
Accuracy.plsda
## [1] 0.6162791

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.plsda <- roc(Ytest_numeric, prob_M)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.plsda, type = "s")

plsda.AUC = auc(roc_obj.plsda)
plsda.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9928

Calibration Plot:

plsda.calplot<-valProbggplot(prob_M, Ytest_numeric)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
plsda.calplot

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = prob_M, y = Ytest_numeric)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##          Dxy      C (ROC)           R2            D     D:Chi-sq          D:p 
##    0.9856007    0.9928004    0.9065375    1.0613122  183.5456939    0.0000000 
##            U     U:Chi-sq          U:p            Q        Brier    Intercept 
##    0.5867494  102.9209037    0.0000000    0.4745627    0.1169014   -0.4280337 
##        Slope         Emax Brier scaled         Eavg          ECI 
##    8.6409640    0.4062775    0.4812641    0.2759094    8.3363382

Linear Discriminant Analysis:

lda.fit <- lda(malignant ~., data = BCW, subset = train.BCW)
lda.pred <- predict(lda.fit, BCW.test)
lda.class <- lda.pred$class
lda.probs <- lda.pred$posterior
prob_M <- lda.probs[, 2]

Brier’s Score:

lda_Brier <- brier_score(V2.test, prob_M)
lda_Brier
## [1] 0.01692952

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy Score:

BCW.lda.pred <- rep(0, nrow(BCW.test)) #size of the test set.
BCW.lda.pred[prob_M > .3] <- 1 #.5threshold for classification
table(BCW.lda.pred, V2.test)
##             V2.test
## BCW.lda.pred   0   1
##            0 112   2
##            1   1  57
Accuracy.lda = mean(BCW.lda.pred == V2.test)
Accuracy.lda
## [1] 0.9825581

Assessing Results:

summary(prob_M)  
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## 4.000e-08 9.648e-05 3.125e-03 3.228e-01 9.945e-01 1.000e+00
hist(prob_M)

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.lda <- roc(V2.test, prob_M)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.lda, type = "s")

lda.AUC = auc(roc_obj.lda)
lda.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9988

Calibration Plot:

lda.calplot<-valProbggplot(prob_M, V2.test)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
lda.calplot

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = prob_M, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##          Dxy      C (ROC)           R2            D     D:Chi-sq          D:p 
##   0.99760012   0.99880006   0.97222187   1.21001534 209.12263806   0.00000000 
##            U     U:Chi-sq          U:p            Q        Brier    Intercept 
##   0.03061056   7.26501707   0.02644975   1.17940477   0.01692952   0.94626516 
##        Slope         Emax Brier scaled         Eavg          ECI 
##   2.10703469   0.45084140   0.92487731   0.02769997   0.51928300

Naive Bayes

nb.fit <- naiveBayes(malignant ~ ., data = BCW, subset = train.BCW) #TEST/ TRAIN SPLIT

Brier’s Score:

nb.pred <- predict(nb.fit, BCW.test, type = "raw")  #again predicted on the test set 
nb.probs <- nb.pred[, 2]#extracts column of probability=malignant 
NB_Brier = brier_score(V2.test, nb.probs)#Ensure factors are 1 & 0. 
NB_Brier
## [1] 0.03812851

Confusion Matrix & Accuracy:

nb.class= rep(0, nrow(BCW.test))  
nb.class[nb.probs > 0.3] = 1  
nb.class <- factor(nb.class, levels = c(0,1))   
table(nb.class, V2.test)
##         V2.test
## nb.class   0   1
##        0 109   3
##        1   4  56
NB.Accuracy <- mean(nb.class == V2.test)
NB.Accuracy
## [1] 0.9593023

Assessing Results:

summary(nb.probs)  
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  0.0000  0.0000  0.0000  0.3486  1.0000  1.0000
hist(nb.probs)

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.NB <- roc(V2.test, nb.probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.NB, type = "s", col = "blue")

NB.AUC = auc(roc_obj.NB)
NB.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9951

Calibration Curve:

valProbggplot(nb.probs, V2.test)
## Warning in valProbggplot(nb.probs, V2.test): 44 observations deleted from
## logistic calibration due to probs. of 0 or 1

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = nb.probs, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##           Dxy       C (ROC)            R2             D      D:Chi-sq 
##  9.610619e-01  9.805310e-01  7.444892e-01  4.751329e-01  6.181702e+01 
##           D:p             U      U:Chi-sq           U:p             Q 
##  3.774758e-15  5.145669e-01  6.786456e+01  1.887379e-15 -3.943396e-02 
##         Brier     Intercept         Slope          Emax  Brier scaled 
##  5.123519e-02 -1.233531e+00  1.653276e-01  4.219558e-01  7.726501e-01 
##          Eavg           ECI 
##  3.395428e-02  1.939516e-01

K-NN

set.seed(123)
k.values <- 1:20 #k can be 1 to 20
cv.error <- rep(NA, 20) #stores misclassification error
for(i in k.values){
  pred <- knn.cv(train = X,cl = Ytrain,k = i) #leave-one-out CV 
  cv.error[i] <- mean(pred != Ytrain) #stores misclassification erro
}
best.k <- which.min(cv.error)
best.k
## [1] 7

Brier Score:

knn.fit <- knn(X, XTest, Ytrain, k = 3, prob = TRUE)
knn.probs <- attr(knn.fit, "prob") #knn for classification couldnt use predict? 
prob_M <- ifelse(knn.fit == 1, knn.probs, 1 - knn.probs)
knn_Brier = brier_score(Ytest_numeric, prob_M)
knn_Brier
## [1] 0.02260982

Accuracy & Confusion Matrix:

knn.class= rep(0, nrow(BCW.test))  
knn.class[prob_M > 0.3] = 1  
table(knn.class, Ytest_numeric)
##          Ytest_numeric
## knn.class   0   1
##         0 105   1
##         1   8  58
knn.Accuracy = mean(knn.class==Ytest_numeric)
knn.Accuracy
## [1] 0.9476744
summary(prob_M)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  0.0000  0.0000  0.0000  0.3295  1.0000  1.0000
hist(prob_M)

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.knn<- roc(Ytest, prob_M)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.knn, type = 's')

knn.AUC = auc(roc_obj.knn)
knn.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9891

Calibration Curve:

knn.calplot<-valProbggplot(prob_M, Ytest_numeric)
## Warning in valProbggplot(prob_M, Ytest_numeric): 155 observations deleted from
## logistic calibration due to probs. of 0 or 1
knn.calplot 

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = prob_M, y = Ytest_numeric)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##          Dxy      C (ROC)           R2            D     D:Chi-sq          D:p 
##  0.666666667  0.833333333  0.620040123  0.565716827 10.617186056  0.001120414 
##            U     U:Chi-sq          U:p            Q        Brier    Intercept 
##          Inf          Inf  0.000000000         -Inf  0.228758170  0.349415168 
##        Slope         Emax Brier scaled         Eavg          ECI 
## 11.677976970  0.548590453 -0.015086501  0.023088983  0.442946109

Random Forest:

#As now will be doing classification tasks so should be a factor. 
rf.fit <- randomForest(as.factor(malignant) ~ ., data = BCW, subset =train.BCW, mtry = 5, importance = TRUE)
varImpPlot(rf.fit)

CV for m:

set.seed(321)
mtry.values <- c(2, 5, 8, 10, 15)

mtry.errors <- sapply(mtry.values, function(m){
  fit <- randomForest(as.factor(malignant) ~., data = BCW[train.BCW, ], mtry = m, ntree = 500)
  return(fit$err.rate[500, "OOB"])  
})

best.mtry <- mtry.values[which.min(mtry.errors)]
best.mtry
## [1] 8

Brier Score:

rf.probs <- predict(rf.fit, newdata = BCW.test, type = "prob")
prob_M <- rf.probs[, 2]
rf_Brier <- brier_score(V2.test, prob_M)
rf_Brier
## [1] 0.01869921

Accuracy & Confusion Matrix:

rf.class= rep(0, nrow(BCW.test))  
rf.class[prob_M > 0.3] = 1
rf.class = factor(rf.class, levels = c(0,1))
table(rf.class, V2.test)
##         V2.test
## rf.class   0   1
##        0 105   0
##        1   8  59
rf.Accuracy = mean(rf.class==V2.test)
rf.Accuracy
## [1] 0.9534884

Assessing Predictions:

summary(prob_M)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  0.0000  0.0080  0.0430  0.3623  0.9470  1.0000
hist(prob_M)

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.rf<- roc(V2.test, prob_M)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.rf, type = 's')

rf.AUC = auc(roc_obj.rf)
rf.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9991

Calibration Curve:

rf.calplot<-valProbggplot(prob_M, V2.test)
## Warning in valProbggplot(prob_M, V2.test): 37 observations deleted from
## logistic calibration due to probs. of 0 or 1
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
rf.calplot 

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = prob_M, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##           Dxy       C (ROC)            R2             D      D:Chi-sq 
##  9.968421e-01  9.984211e-01  9.672327e-01  1.133140e+00  1.539739e+02 
##           D:p             U      U:Chi-sq           U:p             Q 
##  0.000000e+00  1.038703e-01  1.602249e+01  3.317108e-04  1.029270e+00 
##         Brier     Intercept         Slope          Emax  Brier scaled 
##  2.382418e-02 -4.539716e-01  5.803427e+00  4.023425e-01  8.942831e-01 
##          Eavg           ECI 
##  4.499200e-02  6.481701e-01

Boosting:

I trialed different Boosting Models: with different number of trees, different shrinkage and different interaction depths.

set.seed(321)
boost.fit1000 <- gbm(malignant ~., data = BCW[train.BCW, ],distribution = "bernoulli",n.trees = 1000,interaction.depth = 4,shrinkage = 0.01,cv.folds = 5)

best.trees <- gbm.perf(boost.fit1000, method = "cv")

best.trees
## [1] 693

Shrinkage:

set.seed(321)
shrinkage.values <- c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2) 

shrinkage.errors <- sapply(shrinkage.values, function(s){
  fit <- gbm(malignant ~ ., data = BCW[train.BCW, ], distribution = "bernoulli", n.trees = 585,    interaction.depth = 4,shrinkage = s, cv.folds = 5) 
  return(min(fit$cv.error))       
})

best.shrinkage <- shrinkage.values[which.min(shrinkage.errors)]
best.shrinkage
## [1] 0.05

Interaction Depth:

set.seed(321)
id <- c(2, 3, 4, 5) 

depth.errors <- sapply(id, function(d){
  fit <- gbm(malignant ~ ., data = BCW[train.BCW, ], distribution = "bernoulli", n.trees = 585,    interaction.depth = d,shrinkage = 0.2, cv.folds = 5)
  return(min(fit$cv.error))
})

best.depth <- id[which.min(depth.errors)]
best.depth
## [1] 3

Brier Score:

boost.fit <- gbm(malignant ~ ., data = BCW[train.BCW, ], distribution = "bernoulli", n.trees = 585,    interaction.depth = 4,shrinkage = 0.02) #optimum fit
summary(boost.fit)

##                                     var    rel.inf
## Perimeter_extreme     Perimeter_extreme 23.8482028
## Nconcave_extreme       Nconcave_extreme 21.2544221
## Nconcave_mean             Nconcave_mean 18.9504109
## Radius_extreme           Radius_extreme  8.3899592
## Area_extreme               Area_extreme  7.4891415
## Texture_extreme         Texture_extreme  3.5556176
## Concavity_extreme     Concavity_extreme  3.3722925
## Area_se                         Area_se  2.4516990
## Texture_mean               Texture_mean  1.8969707
## Symmetry_extreme       Symmetry_extreme  1.0521962
## Perimeter_mean           Perimeter_mean  0.8019351
## Smoothness_extreme   Smoothness_extreme  0.7741592
## Concavity_mean           Concavity_mean  0.6912447
## Compactness_mean       Compactness_mean  0.6823109
## Area_mean                     Area_mean  0.5907655
## Compactness_se           Compactness_se  0.5416832
## Perimeter_se               Perimeter_se  0.4531463
## Symmetry_se                 Symmetry_se  0.4208346
## Compactness_extreme Compactness_extreme  0.4131588
## Radius_se                     Radius_se  0.3182593
## Radius_mean                 Radius_mean  0.3047414
## Nconcave_se                 Nconcave_se  0.2642395
## Smoothness_mean         Smoothness_mean  0.2557540
## Fractaldim_se             Fractaldim_se  0.2492437
## Texture_se                   Texture_se  0.2332952
## Symmetry_mean             Symmetry_mean  0.1695962
## Fractaldim_mean         Fractaldim_mean  0.1605831
## Fractaldim_extreme   Fractaldim_extreme  0.1383943
## Smoothness_se             Smoothness_se  0.1381790
## Concavity_se               Concavity_se  0.1375633
boost.probs <- predict(boost.fit, newdata = BCW.test, type = "response") #vector of probs 
## Using 585 trees...
boost_Brier <- brier_score(V2.test, boost.probs)
boost_Brier
## [1] 0.00915054

Accuracy & Confusion Matrix:

boost.class= rep(0, nrow(BCW.test))  
boost.class[boost.probs > 0.3] = 1  
boost.class = factor(boost.class, levels = c(0, 1))
table(boost.class, V2.test)
##            V2.test
## boost.class   0   1
##           0 109   0
##           1   4  59
boost.Accuracy = mean(boost.class==V2.test)
boost.Accuracy
## [1] 0.9767442

Assessing Results:

summary(boost.probs)  
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## 4.999e-05 3.729e-04 2.005e-03 3.530e-01 9.974e-01 9.999e-01
hist(boost.probs)

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.boost<- roc(V2.test, boost.probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.boost, type = 's')

boost.AUC = auc(roc_obj.boost)
boost.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9997

Calibration Curve:

boost.calplot<-valProbggplot(boost.probs, V2.test)
boost.calplot 

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = boost.probs, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##           Dxy       C (ROC)            R2             D      D:Chi-sq 
##   0.999400030   0.999700015   0.982882271   1.236380776 213.657493438 
##           D:p             U      U:Chi-sq           U:p             Q 
##   0.000000000   0.005874158   3.010355103   0.221977878   1.230506618 
##         Brier     Intercept         Slope          Emax  Brier scaled 
##   0.009150540  -0.779631275   1.823190625   0.323070904   0.959395594 
##          Eavg           ECI 
##   0.013658979   0.154239538

Bayesian Additive Regression Trees

bart.fit <- pbart(X, Ytrain_numeric, x.test = XTest)
## *****Into main of pbart
## *****Data:
## data:n,p,np: 397, 30, 172
## y1,yn: 1, 1
## x1,x[n*p]: 1.096100, -0.973122
## xp1,xp[np*p]: 1.828212, -0.750546
## *****Number of Trees: 50
## *****Number of Cut Points: 100 ... 100
## *****burn and ndpost: 100, 1000
## *****Prior:mybeta,alpha,tau: 2.000000,0.950000,0.212132
## *****binaryOffset: -0.291354
## *****Dirichlet:sparse,theta,omega,a,b,rho,augment: 0,0,1,0.5,1,30,0
## *****nkeeptrain,nkeeptest,nkeeptreedraws: 1000,1000,1000
## *****printevery: 100
## *****skiptr,skipte,skiptreedraws: 1,1,1
## 
## MCMC
## done 0 (out of 1100)
## done 100 (out of 1100)
## done 200 (out of 1100)
## done 300 (out of 1100)
## done 400 (out of 1100)
## done 500 (out of 1100)
## done 600 (out of 1100)
## done 700 (out of 1100)
## done 800 (out of 1100)
## done 900 (out of 1100)
## done 1000 (out of 1100)
## time: 1s
## check counts
## trcnt,tecnt: 1000,1000

Brier Score:

prob_M <- bart.fit$prob.test.mean
bart_Brier <- brier_score(V2.test, prob_M)
bart_Brier 
## [1] 0.01775927

Accuracy & Confusion Matrix:

bart.class <- factor(ifelse(prob_M > 0.5, 1, 0), levels = c(0, 1))
table(bart.class, V2.test)
##           V2.test
## bart.class   0   1
##          0 110   1
##          1   3  58
Accuracy.bart <- mean(bart.class == V2.test.factor)
Accuracy.bart
## [1] 0.9767442

Assessing Results:

summary(prob_M)  
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## 0.001338 0.010677 0.068716 0.362285 0.934337 0.999384
hist(prob_M)

Roc Curve:

roc_obj.bart<- roc(V2.test, prob_M)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_obj.bart, type = 's')

bart.AUC = auc(roc_obj.bart)
bart.AUC
## Area under the curve: 0.9994

Calibration Curve:

bart.calplot<-valProbggplot(prob_M, V2.test)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
bart.calplot 

## Call:
## valProbggplot(p = prob_M, y = V2.test)
## 
## A 95% confidence interval is given for the calibration intercept, calibration slope and c-statistic. 
## 
##           Dxy       C (ROC)            R2             D      D:Chi-sq 
##  9.988001e-01  9.994000e-01  9.780119e-01  1.224249e+00  2.115708e+02 
##           D:p             U      U:Chi-sq           U:p             Q 
##  0.000000e+00  8.716137e-02  1.699176e+01  2.043088e-04  1.137088e+00 
##         Brier     Intercept         Slope          Emax  Brier scaled 
##  1.775927e-02 -4.234182e-01  4.617886e+00  3.627582e-01  9.211954e-01 
##          Eavg           ECI 
##  4.680826e-02  6.591912e-01

Conclusion:

Brier Scores:

brier_scores <- data.frame(Model = c("GLM", "Best Subset Model", "Ridge Model", "Lasso Model", 'PCA Model', 'LDA Model', 'Naive Bayes', 'K-NN'), Brier_Score = c(GLM_Brier, step_Brier, Ridge_Brier, lasso_Brier, plsda_Brier, lda_Brier, NB_Brier, knn_Brier)
)

brier_scores %>%
  kable(caption = "Brier Scores for Different Models", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
Brier Scores for Different Models
Model Brier_Score
GLM 0.035
Best Subset Model 0.023
Ridge Model 0.019
Lasso Model 0.019
PCA Model 0.117
LDA Model 0.017
Naive Bayes 0.038
K-NN 0.023

Accuracy Scores:

Accuracy_scores <- data.frame(Model = c("GLM", "Best SUbset", "Ridge Model", "Lasso Model", 'PCA Model', 'LDA Model', 'Naive Bayes', 'K-NN'), Accuracy_Score = c(GLM.Accuracy,Accuracy.step, Accuracy.Ridge, Accuracy.lasso, Accuracy.plsda, Accuracy.lda, NB.Accuracy, knn.Accuracy)
)

Accuracy_scores %>%
  kable(caption = "Accuracy Scores for Different Models", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
Accuracy Scores for Different Models
Model Accuracy_Score
GLM 0.965
Best SUbset 0.977
Ridge Model 0.965
Lasso Model 0.983
PCA Model 0.616
LDA Model 0.983
Naive Bayes 0.959
K-NN 0.948

AUC Scores:

AUC_scores <- data.frame(Model = c("GLM", "Best SUbset", "Ridge Model", "Lasso Model", 'PCA Model', 'LDA Model', 'Naive Bayes', 'K-NN'), AUC_Score = c(GLM.AUC,step.AUC, Ridge.AUC, lasso.AUC, plsda.AUC, lda.AUC, NB.AUC, knn.AUC)
)

AUC_scores %>%
  kable(caption = "AUC Scores for Different Models", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
AUC Scores for Different Models
Model AUC_Score
GLM 0.965
Best SUbset 0.978
Ridge Model 0.999
Lasso Model 0.999
PCA Model 0.993
LDA Model 0.999
Naive Bayes 0.995
K-NN 0.989

Get all on one calibration & roc curves?

plot(roc_obj.glm, type = "s", col = "blue")
lines(roc_obj.step, type = "s", col = "red") #change the names of the rocs to the datas
lines(roc_obj.ridge, type = "s", col = "purple")
lines(roc_obj.lasso, type = "s", col = "green")
lines(roc_obj.plsda, type = "s", col = "yellow")
lines(roc_obj.lda, type = "s", col = "orange")
lines(roc_obj.NB, type = "s", col = "pink")
lines(roc_obj.knn, type = "s", col = "brown")

Clinical Usefullness:

df_dca <- data.frame(
  V2 = V2.test,
  lasso = as.vector(lasso.probs)
)

dcurves::dca(
  V2 ~ lasso,
  data = df_dca,
  thresholds = seq(0, 0.35, 0.01),
  label = list(BCWpred = "Prediction Model")
)
## Assuming '1' is [Event] and '0' is [non-Event]
## Printing with `plot(x, type = 'net_benefit', smooth = FALSE, show_ggplot_code = FALSE)`

dca_results <- dcurves::dca(
  V2 ~ lasso,
  data = df_dca,
  as_probability = "lasso",
  thresholds = c(0.05, 0.35, 0.75)
)
## Assuming '1' is [Event] and '0' is [non-Event]
dca_results %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  select(label, threshold, net_benefit) %>%
  gt::gt() %>%
  gt::fmt_percent(columns = threshold, decimals = 0) %>%
  gt::fmt(columns = net_benefit, fns = function(x) style_sigfig(x, digits = 3)) %>%
  gt::cols_label(
    label = "Strategy",
    threshold = "Decision Threshold",
    net_benefit = "Net Benefit"
  ) %>%
  gt::cols_align("left", columns = label)
Strategy Decision Threshold Net Benefit
Treat All 5% 0.308
Treat All 35% -0.011
Treat All 75% -1.63
Treat None 5% 0.000
Treat None 35% 0.000
Treat None 75% 0.000
lasso 5% 0.336
lasso 35% 0.331
lasso 75% 0.314
